wfg_ltv_econ.workerPoolConditionDesc: Smaller colonies have mostly workers, while larger colonies house more families and independent residents, limiting the labor controlled by the government.
wfg_ltv_econ.REDISTRIBUTION_DISCLAIMER: * If the redistribute credits option is enabled, credit reserves may differ from the calculated net income due to internal reallocation.
wfg_ltv_econ.incomeReportExportsDesc: Income from exports by this outpost or colony. Smuggling exports do not produce income. In-faction imports are %1$s cheaper than normal
wfg_ltv_econ.incomeReportWagesDesc: Monthly wages for workers at this colony.
wfg_ltv_econ.incomeReportPlayerCutDesc1: The ratio of monthly profits that get automatically transferred to you: %1$s.
wfg_ltv_econ.incomeReportPlayerCutDesc2: The effective value can be below the chosen value if the colony is in debt.
wfg_ltv_econ.incomeReportPlayerCutDesc3: All income values are modified by this value
wfg_ltv_econ.comCellRaidObjectiveStockShortages: Shortfall between the target and current stockpiles.
wfg_ltv_econ.weaponsRaidObjectiveTp1: Ship weapons, fighter LPCs, and hullmod specs. Availability is based on the "%1$s" commodity.
wfg_ltv_econ.weaponsRaidObjectiveTp2: The colony faction's doctrine affects the number of weapons vs fighter LPCs acquired. Higher ship quality increases the probability of finding modspecs..
wfg_ltv_econ.weaponsRaidObjectiveTp3: This colony does not have heavy industry or a military presence and has no access to high-tier ship equipment.
wfg_ltv_econ.weaponsRaidObjectiveTp4: This colony has heavy industry and high-tier equipment may be found.
wfg_ltv_econ.weaponsRaidObjectiveTp5: This colony has a military presence and high-tier equipment may be found.
wfg_ltv_econ.industryTooltipOperationsDisruptedTxt: Operations disrupted! %%1$s %1$s until return to normal function.
wfg_ltv_econ.industryTooltipQueuedSection1: Click to remove or adjust position in queue
wfg_ltv_econ.industryTooltipQueuedSection2: Currently queued for construction. Does not have any impact on the colony.
wfg_ltv_econ.industryTooltipNormalSection1: Currently under construction and not producing anything or providing other benefits.
wfg_ltv_econ.industryTooltipIOValuesDisclaimer: *Shown production and demand values are already adjusted based on current market size and local conditions.
wfg_ltv_econ.industryTooltipNoAICoresInstalledTxt: No AI core currently assigned. Click to assign an AI core from your cargo.
wfg_ltv_econ.industryTooltipAlphaCoreDesc3: Reduces demand by %1$s. Reduces upkeep cost by %2$s. Increases production by %3$s. All modifiers are multiplicative
wfg_ltv_econ.industryTooltipBetaCoreDesc3: Reduces demand by %1$s. Reduces upkeep cost by %2$s. All modifiers are multiplicative
wfg_ltv_econ.industryTooltipGammaCoreDesc3: Reduces demand by %1$s. All modifiers are multiplicative
wfg_ltv_econ.industryTooltipImprovementDisclaimer: Each improvement made at a colony doubles the number of %1$s required to make an additional improvement.
wfg_ltv_econ.marketPolicyExpandShipyardsDisclaimer: This policy can only be activated at the faction capital, and resources are drawn from its stockpiles.
wfg_ltv_econ.marketPolicyCooldownTxt: Policy currently on cooldown. Available in %1$d days.
wfg_ltv_econ.intelBombardTpTxt1: %%1$s was %2$s. Destroyed %%3$s of local stockpiles and reduced population health and happiness by %%4$s.
wfg_ltv_econ.intelPolicyTpTxt1: The policy %1$s is now available on %2$s.
wfg_ltv_econ.assignWorkersTpTxt1: Assign idle workers to increase this industry's output.
wfg_ltv_econ.assignWorkersTpTxt2: The number of workers that can be assigned to an industry is determined by the colony size, and certain industries have a natural limit on how many workers they can employ. Currently, there are %1$s workers employed in %2$s.
wfg_ltv_econ.serviceSectorTpTxt1: Assign workers to public services that improve stability, growth, trade efficiency, and planetary defense.
wfg_ltv_econ.serviceSectorTpTxt2: The number of workers available for public services depends on colony size and how many are already employed in other industries. Currently, %1$s workers serve in civil departments across the colony.
wfg_ltv_econ.warningTxtGrandColonies: LTV-Economy already has a scroll bar for industries. Using Grand.Colonies will break the game.
wfg_ltv_econ.warningTxtAstralAscension: Astral Ascension is not supported by LTV-Economy. Use at your own risk.
wfg_ltv_econ.warningTxtAOTD: Ashes of the Domain has its own economy rework. LTV-Economy is not designed to be compatible with AoTD and never will be.
wfg_ltv_econ.submarketEconCommodityOnlyTxt: Can only store economy relevant commodities
wfg_ltv_econ.submarketStockpilesTpTxt1: Commodities can be deposited into or withdrawn from the stockpiles. The displayed cargo shows up to the stockpile limit of %1$s or the amount currently stored, whichever is lower. Contributions update the colony's reserves immediately, and the visible cargo reflects the current stockpile limits.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiComSelectionTxt1: Ctrl + Click to view global market info
wfg_ltv_econ.uiComSelectionTxt2: Must be in range of a comm relay to view global market info
wfg_ltv_econ.uiLiftEmbargoTxt: Lift the embargo on %1$s? Colonies may resume trade.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiImposeEmbargoTxt: Impose an embargo on %1$s? Colonies cannot trade with this faction while the embargo is active. This will negatively impact relations.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtRefreshMarkets: Synchronizes EconomyEngine markets with vanilla Economy markets. Does extra checks to make sure no data is missing.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtAllocateWorkers: Recreates the production matrix, and recalculates worker assignments. Also does a fake advance.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtFakeAdvance: Recomputes the economy data without worker assignments.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtRealAdvance: Debug only. Advances the economy by one tick (day) without worker assignments.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtLogAllComCells: Logs all the information about the CommodityCell for each cell
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtLogEconInfo: Logs all the information about the Economy
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtLogIndIO: Logs the maps used by IndustryIOs to manage industry inputs and outputs
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtAllocateShips: Plans and allocates ship orders for Hegemony's FactionShipInventory
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtGlobalProd: The combined daily output of %1$s across all colonies and the informals in the Sector. Represents active industrial and informal production, excluding existing stockpiles.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtGlobalDemand: The combined daily demand of all colonies for %1$s. Demand reflects how much the sector needs to maintain standard production, growth, and stability.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtGlobalSurplus: The amount of %1$s that the sector produced beyond what was demanded. A higher surplus means that, even after all importing markets had their needs filled, some production still remained unused.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtGlobalDeficit: Global deficit represents the total amount of demand that remained unfulfilled on the previous day. This value does not track shortages in stockpiles and only measures demand that was not supplied on the previous day. A colony may have large reserves and still contribute to the global deficit if trade routes could not deliver enough units in time.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtSectorTradeVolume: Total units of %1$s traded across the sector for the last %2$s days, including both in-faction and global cargo.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtSectorTradeValue: The total monetary value (in credits) of all %1$s trades across the entire sector for the last %2$s days. This includes both in-faction and global trade, calculated using the prices at which commodities were exchanged.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtGlobalAvgPrice: The average price of %1$s across all markets in the sector on the previous day, weighted by the quantities traded. This provides a sector-wide benchmark price, reflecting both in-faction and out-of-faction transactions.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtGlobalStockpiles: Shows the total amount of %1$s currently stored across all markets in the sector. This value reflects available stock and does not account for daily production or consumption. High stockpiles indicate abundance, while low stockpiles signal scarcity and potential trade opportunities.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtGlobalWorkersAllocated: Displays the total number of workers currently assigned to producing %1$s across all markets in the sector .Workers are counted based on the output of industries producing this commodity, not the industry as a whole.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtGlobalExporters: The total count of markets in the sector that globally exported %1$s on the previous trade cycle. Informal trade excluded.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtGlobalImporters: The total count of markets in the sector that globally imported %1$s on the previous trade cycle. Informal trade excluded.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtInformalProd: The amount of %1$s produced by the informal sector on the previous day.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtGlobalExportShareFaction: Shows the percentage of total exports controlled by factions and informals. Percentages do not include in-faction trade. Values are based on the previous trade cycle.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtGlobalImportShareFaction: Shows the percentage of total imports made by factions and informals. Percentages do not include in-faction trade. Values are based on the previous trade cycle.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtInFactionTradeRatio: Shows the proportion of this commodity's total exports that are traded outside the producing faction (%1$s) versus exports consumed within the same faction (%2$s). Values are based on the previous day.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtSectorPopBreakdown1: Shows the population of the sector with the share of each faction.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtSectorWorkforceBreakdown: Shows the worker population of the sector with the share of each faction.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtSectorEmploymentBreakdown: Ratio of employed and unemployed workers of the sector.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiDiscardHullsTxt: Discard all hulls? A portion of allocated resources will be recovered as scrap metal.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiScuttleVesselsTxt: Scuttle vessels? A portion of the hull materials will be recovered as scrap metal.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtActiveOrders: Number of ships on the production queue.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtHullsTotalBuildTime: Estimated number of days needed to construct all queued vessels assuming all the assembly lines are utilized.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtHullsAssemblyLines: Number of assembly lines that can concurrently produce ships. Can be increased with policies.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiShipProdCheckboxTxt: Auto-manage ship construction to meet trade demands
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtPendingOrders: The number of ship orders awaiting resource allocation. Once the required commodities are available, they will be moved to the production queue.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTitleTotalCreditCostPendingOrders: The sum of credits required to fulfill all pending orders. This amount will be deducted from the faction's capital as each order is activated.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtActiveHullsTotalBuildTime: The cumulative number of days needed to construct all pending vessels, assuming they are built sequentially. Actual completion may be shorter (up to %1$s production lines may operate concurrently).
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtShipsCommodityCost: The total amount of the "Ships" commodity required across all pending orders. This resource represents the hull components and prefabricated sections needed for construction.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtCargoCapacity: The total cargo space available across all faction ships, including both idle vessels and those currently assigned to missions.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtFuelCapacity: Total fuel tankage of the faction's ships, including both idle vessels and those currently assigned to missions.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtCrewCapacity: Maximum crew berths across all ships. Idle capacity represents space for additional personnel beyond skeleton crew.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtCombatPower: Total combat strength of the ships. Idle power is available for future escort assignments.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiDailyMaintenanceTpStr: Supplies consumed per day to keep all ships operational. Idle ships incur reduced maintenance (%1$s).
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtCrewComplement: Total crew employed across all ships, including maintenance crews for idle ships and full complements for active vessels.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtMonthlyCrewWages1: Total credits paid to all crew per month. Idle crew receive reduced wages (%1$s).
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtDailyTonnageFilter: Filter trade routes by daily volume (%1$s tonnes)
wfg_ltv_econ.factionInvShipTpTxt1: The faction inventory owns a total of %%1$s %1$s ships, %%2$s of which are in-use and the rest (%%3$s) are idle. It takes %%4$s tons of supplies per day to maintain the ships, where the maintenance costs of idle ships are reduced by %%5$s.
wfg_ltv_econ.factionInvShipTpTxt2: It takes %%1$s crew to maintain the ships throughout the month. Each active crew member is paid %%2$s per month, whereas the wages of idle ship crew are reduced by %%3$s, for a total of %%4$s. The %1$s boasts a combat power of %%5$s for a total power of %%6$s.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtFactionInvShipWidgetRemove: %1$s + %2$s to remove one. %3$s + %2$s to remove ten
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtFactionInvShipWidgetAdd: %1$s + %2$s to open the dialog. %3$s + %2$s to add ten
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtShipOrder1: The %1$s production order will enter the queue once the required resources are allocated. A total cost of %%1$s will be charged to the faction's capital, %%2$s, and construction is expected to take %%3$s before the ship is ready for service.
wfg_ltv_econ.shipProductionItemTpTxt1: The %1$s is being assembled in the shipyards.
wfg_ltv_econ.shipProductionItemTpTxt2: The %1$s awaits its turn in the production queue.
wfg_ltv_econ.shipProductionItemTpTxt3: The production will be completed in %1$s. Cancelling a production line recovers a portion of the expended resources as metal scrap.
wfg_ltv_econ.tradeMissionWidgetTpTxt1: The fleet was assembled using ships from the faction inventory.
wfg_ltv_econ.tradeMissionWidgetTpTxt2: The fleet belongs to an independent captain who was hired to deliver the shipment.
wfg_ltv_econ.tradeMissionWidgetTpTxt3: The fuel for the journey was taken from local stockpiles.
wfg_ltv_econ.tradeMissionWidgetTpTxt4: The fuel was purchased at a premium from independent merchants.
wfg_ltv_econ.tradeMissionWidgetTpTxt5: The %%1$s trade mission from %%2$s to %%3$s is expected to cover a distance of %%4$s in %%5$s and burn %%6$s units of fuel. %1$s The shipment consists primarily of %%7$s and the single most abundant commodity is %%8$s. %2$s The costs incurred for this shipment was %%9$s. %3$s The fleet posesses a combat power of %%10$s.
wfg_ltv_econ.virtualFleetTpTxt: Virtual fleet - hauls cargo and uses resources, but cannot be engaged.
wfg_ltv_econ.activeFleetTpTxt: Active fleet - currently in space and open to interception.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtMonthlyIncomeUpkeep1: The net monthly income of this colony last month was %1$s.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtMonthlyIncomeUpkeep2: The net monthly upkeep for this colony last month was %1$s.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtMonthlyIncomeUpkeep3: These modifiers affect industry income & upkeep only.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiBtnTpTxtPopWorkforceManage: The Statistics Bureau tracks census data, health trends, and workforce allocation across all industries. Policy decrees can be drafted and enacted from this office.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiDialogTpTxtAssignWorkers: Adjust each output's slider to allocate a portion of the market's total workforce. The values represent the percentage of available workers assigned to that output.
wfg_ltv_econ.colonyBalanceTpTxt1: Shows the colony's current credit reserves. These funds cover operating costs, import purchases, and upkeep for industries and structures. A low balance will prevent the activation of certain policies. Too much debt will undesirable effects.
wfg_ltv_econ.colonyBalanceTpTxt2: Colony reserves are separate from your personal credits.
wfg_ltv_econ.playerBalanceTpTxt: Shows your personal credits for transferring funds to or from the colony's reserves.
wfg_ltv_econ.autoTransferTpTxt1: The ratio of monthly profits that get automatically transferred to the player
wfg_ltv_econ.autoTransferTpTxt2: You would receive %1$s from this colony so far this month.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTableBaseProdTpTxt: Theoretical local daily production, assuming no deficits or shortages.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTableRealProdTpTxt: Actual daily production after accounting for stored deficits.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTableBaseBalanceTpTxt: Net daily change in stockpile, ignoring imports or exports.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTableRealBalanceTpTxt:
Net daily change in stockpile, including imports or exports. Does not account for discrete expenditures such as hull production for the faction hangar.

This value may not reflect the effects of imports and exports due to their periodic nature
wfg_ltv_econ.uiShowExcessDeficitTxt: Only show colonies with excess stockpiles or shortages
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtShowExcessDeficit:
Only show colonies that are either suffering from a shortage or have excess stockpiles.

Colonies with excess stockpiles have more of the goods available on the open market and have lower prices.

Colonies with shortages have less or none available for sale, and have higher prices.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtGlobalMarketValue:
Sector-wide spendings for the import of %1$s in the last %%1$s days. Colonies with higher accessibility, faction relations and a shorter distance to customers will have priority when exporting.

The value shown here includes the demand at your colonies, since they must import goods as well. In-faction imports have a %%2$s discount.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtGlobalExports:
The total number of %1$s exported globally by all producing markets in the last trade cycle.

 This figure reflects the total global supply that reaches exportable surplus after local and in-faction demand is met.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtTotalFactionExports:
The total number of units exported to %%1$s, as well as the total exported %%2$s under %1$s control.

Global exports are shaped by the colony's accessibility, its faction relations and other factors.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtFactionMarketShare: Total export market share for %1$s for all colonies under %2$s control.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtMarketShare1:
What percentage of the global market value the colony receives as income from its exports of the commodity.

The market share is affected by the number of units produced and the colony's accessibility.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtMarketShare2:
The portion of the global market value that this colony contributes.

Includes player-controlled colonies.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtCreditHeader1: Last month's income the colony earned from exports.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtCreditHeader2: Last month's expenses the colony spent on imports.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTableAccessibilityTpTxt: A colony's accessibility. Influences trade priority.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiMarketStatusSummaryTxt: %1$s is a size %%1$s %%2$s colony located %2$s. Its current stability is %%1$s.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiBoughtAndSoldForTxt: Can be bought for %1$s and sold for %2$s per unit, assuming a batch of %3$s units traded.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTableQuantityTpTxt1: Units of this commodity exported globally in the last trade cycle.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTableQuantityTpTxt2: Units of this commodity imported globally in the last trade cycle.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtRateOfExploitation: Controls the proportion of worker output the colony retains as profit. A higher value means the colony keeps more of the workers' production, increasing colony income but reducing worker wages. A lower value increases wages but reduces the colony's net income. Extreme values might have unintended consequences.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtMonthlyWages: Total wages paid to workers per month.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtAvgWage: The average monthly income of workers in the colony. Each person spends 1%1$s each month to purchase food.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtWorkforceEmployedTotal: Shows how many workers are currently employed compared to the colony's total labor capacity.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtPolicyWidget2: Not enough market credits to activate this policy.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtLogistics: Tasked with the efficient allocation and transportation of resources, the logistics sector reduces overall resource consumption by %1$s%% (max %2$s%%).
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtHealthcare: The healthcare sector increases life expectancy, which contributes %1$s (max %2$s) growth points. The health of the population is also increased by %3$s (max %4$s) per-day.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtSecurity: The ministry of security is distinct from COMSEC in that it is tasked with ensuring public order, subduing "troublemakers" among the populace and creating a secondary line against invaders. Decreases class consciousness by %1$s (max %2$s) and boosts ground defenses by %3$s (max %4$s).
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtPublicInfo: The public information department broadcasts daily news and educational programmes. Increases social cohesion by %1$s (max %2$s) and accessibility by %3$s (max %4$s).
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtCulture: The ministry of culture organizes social events, funds artistic venues, and supports recreational programs. Increases social cohesion by %1$s (max %2$s), happiness by %3$s (max %4$s), and generates %5$s credits per month from event participation (max %6$s).
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtCohesion: Degree of social cohesion within the population. High cohesion reduces conflict and increases stability.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtClassConsciousness: The population's awareness of exploitation and social hierarchy. Higher values indicate a greater likelihood of collective action. Can be lowered by increasing wages, improving health, raising happiness, or implementing policies that reduce perceived inequities.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtHappiness: Overall happiness and morale of the population. Influenced by health, wages, stability, and social cohesion.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtHealth: Overall health of the population. A higher value indicates better living conditions, food availability, and lower hazard exposure.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiViewGlobalMarketInfo: Click to view global market info
wfg_ltv_econ.uiCannotViewGlobalMarketInfo: Must be in range of a comm relay to view global market info
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtCommodityWidget1: Markets with higher production and accessibility are prioritized for exports and imports.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiDescLegendRow1: Proportion of stockpiles compared to the desired amount.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiDescLegendRow3: Smuggled or produced by an illegal enterprise. No income from exports.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiDescLegendRow4: Local production that could not be exported.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiDescLegendRow9: Excess imports stockpiled for future use.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiDescLegendRow10: Deficit not covered by production or imports.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtIndustryMaxNum1: Maximum number of industries, based on the size of a colony and other factors.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtIndustryMaxNum2: Structures such as spaceports or orbital stations do not count against this limit. Colonies that exceed this limit for any reason have their stability reduced by %1$s.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTableTpTxtEmployment: Fraction of the workers assigned to an output.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtShortHealth: Overall health of the colony's population.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtShortHappiness: Overall happiness and morale of the colony's population.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtShortCohesion: Degree of social cohesion within the colony's population.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtShortClassConsciousness: Colony population's awareness of exploitation and social hierarchy.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtFactionCom: All commodities produced or demanded by your faction.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTableTpTxtStored: Total stored amount across all faction markets.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTableTpTxtDemand: Daily demand across all faction markets.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtFactionProd: Daily production across all faction markets.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtFactionComShare: Fraction of global demand covered by your faction's exports.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTableTpTxtCreditFlow: Credits generated by or spent for this commodity.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTableTpTxtInputOutputBalance: Net daily flow of this commodity for your faction, after production, consumption, and trade.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtAutarky: Fraction of total demand satisfied using only in-faction production. Shows how self-sufficient your faction is for this commodity.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTxtExpandRemotePrice: Expand to see remote price data.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtRemotePrices: Seeing remote price data for various colonies requires being within range of a functional comm relay.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtcargoCom1: All values approximate. Prices do not include tariffs, which can be avoided through black market trade.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtcargoCom2: *Per unit prices assume buying or selling a batch of %1$s units. Each unit bought costs more as the market's supply is reduced, and each unit sold brings in less as demand is fulfilled.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtLedger1: %1$s profitably exported %%1$s units of %2$s and accounted for %%2$s of the global market share. They generated %%3$s last month and %%4$s so far this month.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtLedger2: No local production to export for this trade cycle.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtLedger3: %1$s imported %%1$s units of %2$s and accounted for %%2$s of the global market share. They expended %%3$s last month and %%4$s so far this month.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtLedger4: No local demand or stockpiles full.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtAccessibility1: A colony with high accessibility is able to import and export earlier in the trade cycle, and can in turn claim a greater market share for commodities it exports.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtTradeCycle: Trade fleets are dispatched every %1$s days. The %2$s tab shows data from the most recent trade cycle.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtAccessibility2: Trade fleets first consume fuel from colony stockpiles; if stockpiles are empty, they purchase fuel from independent merchants at %1$s the base cost.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtAccessibility3: Should the controlling faction lack sufficient ships, independent captains are hired instead and charge an additional fee on top of fuel costs. There are currently %1$s active trade missions.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtCustomWorkerAllocationPlanNotice: This is a custom plan. It can be modified or deleted.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtDefaultWorkerAllocationPlanNotice: This is a predefined plan. It cannot be modified or deleted.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTxtPressToSetNotExportableStock: Press to configure locked stock amount
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTransferToFactionInventoryTxt1: Strip this ship of all its hullmods and weapons, and transfer it to the faction hangar. Built-in hullmods that were installed by you grant %1$s.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiTpTransferToFactionInventoryTxt2: This action is permanent and cannot be undone.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiDialogTransferToFactionInventoryTxt: Transfer ship to the faction hangar? It cannot be undone. The transfer will grant you %1$s %2$s. You need %3$s crew for the transfer.
wfg_ltv_econ.uiCheckboxTxtAutoAssignWorkers: Re-allocate workers periodically using the most recently run plan
